1.template: And they had all things {0} among them; therefore there were not rich and poor

1.title: The Nephite utopia
1.text: Within just 36 years of Christ's visit, ALL the people — Nephites and Lamanites — were converted unto the Lord. There were no contentions, and every man dealt justly with his neighbor. Here begins the happiest society in all the Book of Mormon.

1.question: Which great city did the Nephites rebuild after the destruction?

1.template: they did not walk any more after the performances and ordinances of the law of {0}

1.title: The disciples depart
1.text: By the hundredth year, the twelve disciples chosen by Jesus had all gone 'to the paradise of God' — except the three who were to tarry. Others were ordained in their stead, keeping the work alive.

1.template: but they were in one, the children of {0}, and heirs to the kingdom of God

1.title: 'There could not be a happier people'
1.text: The chapter describes the height of unity: no envyings, tumults, lies, or murders; no robbers and no '-ites' — neither Lamanites nor Nephites. All were 'in one, the children of Christ.' The text declares there could not be a happier people created by the hand of God.

1.question: What began to corrupt the people in the year 201?
options: Pride — costly apparel, pearls, and fine things of the world

1.template: and they began to build up churches unto themselves to get {0}

1.question: What happened to the disciples cast into furnaces and dens of beasts?
options: They came forth unharmed; they played with the beasts as a child

1.title: The '-ites' return
1.text: After more than two centuries of unity, the people again divide into '-ites': Nephites, Jacobites, Josephites, Zoramites (the believers) and Lamanites, Lemuelites, Ishmaelites (those who rejected the gospel). These did not 'dwindle' — they wilfully rebelled.

1.template: the wicked part of the people began again to build up the secret oaths and combinations of {0}
Amos died; and his brother, {0}, did keep the record in his stead

1.question: What did Ammaron do in the year 320, constrained by the Holy Ghost?
options: He hid up the sacred records unto the Lord

1.title: The hidden records
1.text: The book ends with Ammaron, constrained by the Holy Ghost, hiding the sacred records in the year 320 — that they might come again to the remnant of the house of Jacob. These are the records young Mormon would later retrieve.
